commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2a1230767174829e1e028f9b916cc639293b1e5e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes systemd-supervised managed update handoffs through `systemd-run --user --scope`, adds exit-code 80 restart handling, changes generated systemd unit restart/kill settings, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/report level: current main uses a detached helper while generated systemd units use `KillMode=control-group`, and the linked issue includes live Linux/systemd failure traces. I did not run a live OpenClaw update in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual maintainer review is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof and upgrade/availability semantics, not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(daemon): escape systemd cgroup for update handoff via systemd-run --scope
target.body:
## Summary

On systemd-supervised installs, clicking "Update now" silently fails: the update handoff helper is created but immediately killed by systemd's `KillMode=control-group` when the gateway unit restarts. The helper never executes, the version stays unchanged, and the UI shows "Update skipped: managed-service-handoff-started."

This PR detects systemd supervision and launches the handoff helper via `systemd-run --user --scope` to escape the gateway's cgroup. Non-systemd supervisors (launchd, etc.) continue using the existing detached-child path.

## Root cause

systemd's `KillMode=control-group` (the default) kills all processes in the service's cgroup on restart — including detached child processes. The handoff helper, despite being `unref()`'d and spawned with `detached: true`, stays in the same cgroup and is terminated before it can run `npm install`.

## Fix

```
systemd-run --user --scope --unit openclaw-update-<handoffId> --collect -- node handoff.cjs handoff.json
```

Key design choice: **`--scope` not `--service`**. This matters because:
- `--scope`: child **inherits the caller's environment** (profile, state dir, config, PATH, proxy, package manager discovery all preserved)
- `--service`: child runs in a **clean systemd environment** (would lose all service env vars)

The `env` object is built from `stripSupervisorHintEnv(process.env)` plus update-specific vars, and passed directly to `spawn()` — identical to the existing non-systemd path. Since `--scope` preserves caller env, no `--setenv` forwarding is needed.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `update-managed-service-handoff.ts` | Detect `supervisor === "systemd"` → spawn via `systemd-run --user --scope` |
| `update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` | 3 new tests: systemd path uses `systemd-run --scope`, non-systemd path uses plain detached node, ENOENT throws |
| `systemd-unit.ts` | Switch to `KillMode=mixed` + `StartLimitBurst=5` + `StartLimitIntervalSec=60` for update-safe restarts |
| `systemd-unit.test.ts` | 1 new test: renders mixed kill mode and update-safe restart limits |
| `process-respawn.ts` | Return `systemd-update-exit-80` detail for systemd supervisor |
| `process-respawn.test.ts` | 1 new test: returns systemd-update-exit-80 detail |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts \
    src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 ✓ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests) 49ms
 ✓ gateway-client update.test.ts (18 tests) 47ms
 ✓ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 615ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update.test.ts (18 tests) 47ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 616ms
 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  74 passed (74)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: systemd `KillMode=control-group` kills detached update handoff helper on gateway restart, preventing self-update.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `2a12307671`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test files to the merge-base (`110042d840`) source. 6 tests fail:

```
 ❯ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests | 1 failed) 5ms
 ❯ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests | 1 failed) 25ms
 ❯ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests | 2 failed) 575ms
 ❯ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests | 2 failed) 575ms

 FAIL  buildSystemdUnit > renders mixed kill mode and update-safe restart limits
 AssertionError: expected '[Unit]\nDescription=...' to contain 'KillMode=mixed'
 Received: KillMode=control-group  ← old default kills handoff helper

 FAIL  respawnGatewayProcessForUpdate > returns systemd-update-exit-80 detail for systemd supervisor
 AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'systemd-update-exit-80'

 FAIL  managed service update handoff > spawns via systemd-run --user --scope when supervisor is systemd
 AssertionError: expected '/usr/local/bin/node' to be 'systemd-run'
 ← base code ignores supervisor param, always spawns node directly

 FAIL  managed service update handoff > throws when systemd-run fails to spawn (ENOENT)
 AssertionError: promise resolved instead of rejecting
 ← base code never calls systemd-run, so no ENOENT path

 Test Files  4 failed (4)
      Tests  6 failed | 32 passed (38)
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/systemd-update-handoff-84068
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts \
  src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 ✓ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests) 49ms
 ✓ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 615ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 616ms
 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  74 passed (74)
   Duration  2.20s
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 74 tests pass. The handoff function now detects `supervisor === "systemd"` and spawns via `systemd-run --user --scope` (escaping the service cgroup), the systemd unit template renders `KillMode=mixed` for update-safe restarts, and `respawnGatewayProcessForUpdate` returns the proper `systemd-update-exit-80` detail.
- **What was not tested**: Live systemd update cycle (requires a Linux server with systemd user session, OpenClaw installed via npm, and a version upgrade available). The cgroup escape mechanism is mock-validated at the spawn API level. Docker process-group isolation was additionally tested to confirm the `detached: true` + `setsid()` mechanism works at the OS level.

Closes #84068
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

| `update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts` | 3 new tests: systemd path uses `systemd-run --scope`, non-systemd path uses plain detached node, ENOENT throws |
| `systemd-unit.ts` | Switch to `KillMode=mixed` + `StartLimitBurst=5` + `StartLimitIntervalSec=60` for update-safe restarts |
| `systemd-unit.test.ts` | 1 new test: renders mixed kill mode and update-safe restart limits |
| `process-respawn.ts` | Return `systemd-update-exit-80` detail for systemd supervisor |
| `process-respawn.test.ts` | 1 new test: returns systemd-update-exit-80 detail |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts \
    src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts \
    src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts
 ✓ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 ✓ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests) 49ms
 ✓ gateway-client update.test.ts (18 tests) 47ms
 ✓ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 615ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update.test.ts (18 tests) 47ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 616ms
 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  74 passed (74)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: systemd `KillMode=control-group` kills detached update handoff helper on gateway restart, preventing self-update.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `2a12307671`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test files to the merge-base (`110042d840`) source. 6 tests fail:

```
 ❯ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests | 1 failed) 5ms
 ❯ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests | 1 failed) 25ms
 ❯ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests | 2 failed) 575ms
 ❯ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests | 2 failed) 575ms

 FAIL  buildSystemdUnit > renders mixed kill mode and update-safe restart limits
 AssertionError: expected '[Unit]\nDescription=...' to contain 'KillMode=mixed'
 Received: KillMode=control-group  ← old default kills handoff helper

 FAIL  respawnGatewayProcessForUpdate > returns systemd-update-exit-80 detail for systemd supervisor
 AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'systemd-update-exit-80'

 FAIL  managed service update handoff > spawns via systemd-run --user --scope when supervisor is systemd
 AssertionError: expected '/usr/local/bin/node' to be 'systemd-run'
 ← base code ignores supervisor param, always spawns node directly

 FAIL  managed service update handoff > throws when systemd-run fails to spawn (ENOENT)
 AssertionError: promise resolved instead of rejecting
 ← base code never calls systemd-run, so no ENOENT path

 Test Files  4 failed (4)
      Tests  6 failed | 32 passed (38)
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/systemd-update-handoff-84068
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts \
  src/daemon/systemd-unit.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ unit-fast systemd-unit.test.ts (4 tests) 2ms
 ✓ infra process-respawn.test.ts (20 tests) 49ms
 ✓ gateway-client update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 615ms
 ✓ gateway-methods update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts (7 tests) 616ms
 Test Files  6 passed (6)
      Tests  74 passed (74)
   Duration  2.20s
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 74 tests pass. The handoff function now detects `supervisor === "systemd"` and spawns via `systemd-run --user --scope` (escaping the service cgroup), the systemd unit template renders `KillMode=mixed` for update-safe restarts, and `respawnGatewayProcessForUpdate` returns the proper `systemd-update-exit-80` detail.
- **What was not tested**: Live systemd update cycle (requires a Linux server with systemd user session, OpenClaw installed via npm, and a version upgrade available). The cgroup escape mechanism is mock-validated at the spawn API level. Docker process-group isolation was additionally tested to confirm the `detached: true` + `setsid()` mechanism works at the OS level.

Closes #84068
